claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To get started, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me begin with a few questions.

**First, could you tell me the name(s) of the child or children you're claiming Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - his name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex.

To assess your eligibility, I need to understand your living situation better.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Next, could you tell me Alex's age? Specifically, how old is he?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's useful information.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that you live in the UK. However, I need to be more specific about your immigration status to properly assess your eligibility.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status (also known as Indefinite Leave to Remain) in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information.

**One more question: Are you currently working or receiving any income? If so, do you know your approximate annual income?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do work, but I'm not sure why you need to know about my income for Child Benefit eligibility?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's a fair question! Actually, you're right to question that. Child Benefit eligibility itself isn't means-tested - it doesn't depend on how much you earn. I apologize for that unnecessary question.

Let me just confirm one final thing to make sure I have all the details I need:

**Is Alex in school, or does he have any special circumstances such as being in hospital, residential care, or receiving full-time education elsewhere?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. He just goes to regular school like any other 8-year-old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Thank you for all that information. I now have what I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules to make my determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I can now provide you with my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (he's 8)
- You live with Alex and are responsible for him
- You live in the UK
- You are a British citizen
- Alex has no special circumstances that would affect his eligibility

You should be able to go ahead and make a claim for Child Benefit. If you need to make a claim, you can do so through the official government channels.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The eligibility criteria are met as follows: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (age 8); (2) You are responsible for Alex as you live with him; (3) You live in the UK; (4) You are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency and immigration status requirements; (5) Alex attends regular school with no special circumstances affecting his eligibility (not in hospital, care, or other special arrangements). All key eligibility criteria are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen living in the UK who is responsible for an 8-year-old child living with you, you meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. There are no circumstances identified that would affect or prevent your eligibility.">

All key eligibility criteria are satisfied.
